item.title: US House passes Ukraine aid and Russia sanctions in defiance of Trump
item.description: The GOP-controlled House approved legislation authorizing military aid for Ukraine and new sanctions on Russia, with 18 Republicans breaking with party leaders in a significant foreign policy rebuke to the president.

item.title: UN nuclear watchdog raises proliferation concerns over Iran sites
item.description: The IAEA expressed concern in a confidential report about lack of access to verify nuclear material at Iranian sites, raising proliferation fears amid ongoing US-Iran tensions.

item.title: Saudi Crown Prince condemns Iranian attacks on Bahrain in call with King Hamad
item.description: Mohammed bin Salman expressed solidarity with Bahrain during a phone call with King Hamad, condemning Iranian attacks and underscoring regional tensions.

item.title: US and Europeans fail to agree on new Bosnia peace deal diplomat
item.description: Washington and European partners could not reach consensus on appointing a new diplomat to oversee the 1995 Dayton peace agreement, highlighting transatlantic diplomatic friction.

item.title: Russia flooding Ukraine with fake migrant stories, disinformation campaign exposed
item.description: A Russian disinformation campaign is spreading false claims about a migrant influx in Ukraine, exploiting social divisions as part of hybrid warfare tactics.

item.title: French jets scrambled 11 times in a week to intercept Russian aircraft over Baltics
item.description: French warplanes were scrambled 11 times in the past week as part of NATOs Baltic Air Policing Mission, with Frances armed forces describing the incursions as a higher-than-usual number of provocations by Russia.

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Cuban President Diaz-Canel and family members
item.description: The United States has imposed sanctions on Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel, his wife and several senior figures, escalating pressure on the communist regime amid Cubas ongoing economic and energy crisis.

item.title: Ukraine Launches Drone Attacks on Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Area
item.description: Russian-installed management at Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant accused Ukraine of deploying more than 20 drones to attack a nearby thermal plant vital to supplying the facility with external power, raising safety concerns at Europe's largest nuclear facility.

item.title: US Journalist Pleads Guilty to Acting as Illegal Chinese Agent
item.description: An American journalist pleaded guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government in a case that highlights Beijing's efforts to infiltrate US media organizations. The Justice Department announced the plea as tensions between Washington and Beijing continue.

item.title: US Sanctions Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel
item.description: The United States imposed sanctions on Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel and four other individuals in the latest move to pressure the island's leadership. The measures come amid ongoing tensions between Washington and Havana.

item.title: Zelenskiy Proposes Face-to-Face Peace Talks with Putin in Open Letter
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy published an open letter to Vladimir Putin proposing direct negotiations to end the war, warning Kyiv stands ready to fight on if talks fail.

item.title: China Releases Satellite Images of Nvidia and Apple HQs
item.description: A Chinese satellite company published bird's-eye photos of Nvidia and Apple headquarters, showcasing routine satellite news capabilities despite being under sanctions.

item.title: China Demands End to US Cuba Blockade, Rejects Terrorism Charges
item.description: China's foreign ministry rejected US claims that Cuba sponsors terrorism and called for an end to sanctions on the island nation.

item.title: Taiwan President Lai Commemorates Tiananmen on 37th Anniversary
item.description: President William Lai urged China to face up to history on the 37th anniversary of the Tiananmen Square crackdown.

item.title: Russia-US Energy and Economy Talks Continue Despite Ukraine Pause
item.description: Putin envoy Kirill Dmitriev confirmed ongoing economic and energy discussions with US counterparts, even as Ukraine peace negotiations remain stalled.

item.title: Taiwan to Expand Anti-Ship Missile Arsenal to Counter Chinese Threat
item.description: Taiwan plans to increase its anti-ship missile arsenal to over 1,800 by early 2029 to enhance defenses against potential blockade or invasion from China.

item.title: Trump Ceasefires Fail to Stop Violence Across Middle East
item.description: Residents in Gaza, southern Lebanon, northern Israel, and Kuwait faced ongoing attacks this week despite multiple US-arranged ceasefires supposedly in effect.

item.title: Putin Faces Competing Visions of War and Peace at Russia Investment Forum
item.description: At Russia's premier annual investment conference in St. Petersburg, Vladimir Putin confronted rival outlines of Russia's future as the Ukraine war continues.

item.title: Hezbollah rejects ceasefire deal as Israel continues Lebanon strikes
item.description: Hezbollah rejected a ceasefire agreement approved by the Lebanese and Israeli governments, while Israel said it would not withdraw from southern Lebanon, complicating U.S. efforts to end fighting.

item.title: Gold rises as Middle East ceasefire hopes pressure dollar
item.description: Gold prices gained more than 1% as oil prices slipped on optimism over a potential end to the Iran conflict, which pressured the dollar and caused bond yields to fall.

item.title: Oil settles 3% lower on hopes for Iran deal after Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: Oil prices fell sharply on investor hopes that an end to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran could reopen the Strait of Hormuz, following a ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon.

item.title: Severe El Nino threatens Asian crops as dry weather disrupts planting
item.description: Dry weather is disrupting crop planting across Asia, raising concerns about food supplies, as an expected severe El Nino weather pattern threatens more damage.

item.title: US explores major expansion of Lebanese army support as key to Hezbollah disarmament
item.description: The United States is considering significantly expanding support for the Lebanese Armed Forces as part of a broader strategy to facilitate Hezbollah disarmament, marking a potential shift in US approach to the Lebanon conflict.

item.title: China orders state-owned enterprises to double spending on basic research
item.description: Chinese regulators have directed central state-owned enterprises to double their investment in basic research, aiming to make them globally influential technology sources in 10 strategic sectors within a decade.

item.title: Iran supreme leader claims decisive blow against US and Israel amid mixed signals on talks
item.description: A statement from Iran supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei asserted that the US and Israel had been hit by a decisive blow, as mixed signals emerged about the prospects for a negotiated end to the conflict.
